entries.evidence_summary: Authority is expressed as an operative precedence chain led by the COO controller and active runtime contract, with docs separated under reference evidence.
Material pushback is defined once in guardrails as unresolved blocking or major findings and reused consistently in freeze and terminal-state predicates.
Outputs cleanly separate canonical artifacts from run-scoped evidence and bind each class to explicit path conventions and promotion timing.
Frozen, frozen_with_conditions, pushback, blocked, and resume_required each have explicit, testable trigger predicates and closeout behavior.
The self-check is limited to executed checks only and requires semantic keyword matching evidence for scope coverage rather than literal string matching.
Scope exclusions are deduplicated and Step 1 explicitly normalizes and deduplicates exclusions before generation.
Arbitration has a trigger condition, actor, required evidence fields in result.json, and a constrained effect limited to frozen_with_conditions on minor or suggestion items.
All four required canonical outputs are listed by exact full canonical path, including the validation-contract targets that were missing in the prior self-check.
Completion is defined separately for each terminal state with the exact artifacts and outcomes that make the run complete.
Context gathering is labeled as preconditions before Step 1 and is not mixed into the numbered execution flow.
Carried-forward findings must be addressed by conceptual-group ID, and Step 4.3 binds that requirement to the fix-items map.
Update and fix behavior explicitly appends the canonical decision log and replaces the canonical board summary while preserving supporting run evidence.
The steps section states that all steps are target-state governed behavior, and each numbered step is labeled as target-state behavior.
Per-round artifacts that should persist across rounds are explicitly written every round, while state-specific artifacts are separately gated by terminal-state conditions.
The outputs section provides one authoritative artifact matrix with artifact class, governed roots, concrete canonical targets, and write lifecycle by operation and terminal state.
Local authority order and review semantics are derived from the COO controller, runtime contract, component governance files, and inherited shared review contract rather than reinvented locally.
The role identity describes the enduring mission of the package and does not encode transient run-state or recovery-state details.
The writable surface is explicitly rooted in the runtime contract, names the durable component roots, and constrains canonical writes to the invocation's concrete targets.
The authority chain uses operative sources only, with reference docs classified separately and no run-specific bootstrap context promoted into durable authority.
Governance mechanics are tied to declared inherited contracts and authority sources; no extra local board or freeze policy is introduced outside that set.
The scope section keeps inclusion statements in Use when and exclusions only in Out of scope, with one exclusion statement per concept.
Step 4 defines the governed review round as an ordered sub-sequence and binds each sub-step to its required per-round artifacts.
The role declares the component-local governance files it depends on and states the inherited runtime obligations supplied by the shared review contract.
Required inputs enumerate the request, source refs, reviewer roster, governance files, shared contract reference, governance config, runtime config, and canonical targets needed to execute safely.
